visa_policy.notes: Bolivia uses a 3-tier country grouping: Group I (no visa required for tourism), Group II (standard tourism visa required, 20 calendar days advance), Group III (visa with prior DIGEMIG consultation, 50-60 calendar days advance). In December 2025, Bolivia's Foreign Ministry officially eliminated politically-motivated visa restrictions for 8 countries: USA, South Korea, South Africa, Bulgaria, Malta, Romania, UAE, and Israel (cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2025/12/01/23577). Additional bilateral ordinary-passport visa-free agreements confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo: Honduras (effective 17 March 2024), El Salvador (effective 21 June 2024), Dominican Republic (effective 2 September 2024). Russia has been visa-free since bilateral agreement signed April 2016. MERCOSUR member nationals (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, Venezuela) enjoy 180-day visa-free entry. Mexico also 180-day. Andean Community members (Colombia, Ecuador, Peru) and Chile enjoy 90-day visa-free entry. Visa-on-arrival for Group II nationals ended July 25, 2025 — Group II applicants must now obtain visas in advance. The official online visa application system is at visas.cancilleria.gob.bo. No e-visa or ETA system was found on official sources.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports of any country may enter Bolivia without a visa. Specific bilateral 'Acuerdo de Supresión de Visas para Titulares de Pasaportes Diplomático, Oficiales y de Servicio' confirmed with Equatorial Guinea (signed 23 November 2017, cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2017/11/23/8345) and Switzerland (signed 7 December 2018, cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2018/12/07/7883; stay duration linked to period of functions/assignment). Honduras bilateral agreement (March 2024) also covers diplomatic and official passports. The blanket 90-day rule for all diplomatic/official/service passports is widely reported by Timatic but has not been confirmed verbatim on a directly-accessed official Bolivian government page beyond the specific bilateral agreements.

cbi.notes: Bolivia has no official citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution permits dual citizenship. Citizenship may be obtained after 3 years of continuous legal residency (standard naturalization path, not an investment program). No dedicated CBI program was found on any official Bolivian government source (migracion.gob.bo, cancilleria.gob.bo).

programs.name: Temporary Residency — Determined Purpose (Visa de Objeto Determinado) for Investors/Business
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who invest in Bolivian businesses, establish companies, or conduct business activities may apply for a 'Visa de Objeto Determinado' (Multiple Visa for investors/business). This visa is valid for 1 year with multiple entries; maximum 90-day stays per visit. After 2 years of temporary residency, MERCOSUR nationals can apply for permanent residency. After 3 years of continuous legal residency, any foreign national may apply for Bolivian citizenship. No minimum investment threshold is specified on official sources.

programs.name: Permanent Residency for MERCOSUR Nationals
programs.notes: Nationals of MERCOSUR member and associated states may apply for permanent residency after 2 years of valid temporary residence in Bolivia. Eligibility is governed by the MERCOSUR Residence Agreement. Bolivia formalized full MERCOSUR membership via Law 1567 (July 2024).

agreements: Andean Community (CAN) — free movement of persons under Decisions 503, 545, 878
MERCOSUR — free movement of persons; Bolivia formally acceded via Law 1567 (July 2024)

validation_notes: ADDED Honduras (HND): Ordinary passport visa-free effective March 17, 2024, confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2024/03/16/14347. Was missing from prior record despite being listed in data_quality.gaps.

ADDED Dominican Republic (DOM): Ordinary passport visa-free effective September 2, 2024, confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2024/08/30/17267. Was missing from prior record despite being listed in data_quality.gaps.
ADDED El Salvador (SLV): Ordinary passport visa-free effective June 21, 2024, confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2024/06/18/15822. Was entirely absent from prior record with no mention.
ADDED Russia (RUS): Visa-free since bilateral agreement signed April 2016 (cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2016/04/12/8459), 90-day stay per secondary sources. Was absent from prior record.

REMOVED conditional_access entry for China diplomatic/service passports (30-day rule): source was Wikipedia (source_official=false) with explicit gap-flag. No official Bolivian source confirmed this entry; removed to maintain source integrity policy.

UPDATED conditional_access diplomatic/official/service passport entry: removed the 'except China' carve-out (which was sourced only from Wikipedia/Timatic, not an official source), updated notes to reflect confirmed bilateral agreements with Equatorial Guinea, Switzerland, and Honduras.
NOTED: Visa-on-arrival for Group II nationals ended July 25, 2025 — all Group II applicants must now get visas in advance; added to notes.

NOTED: Embassy EU page (embajadadebolivia.eu/en/turism-visa/) is an older snapshot predating the March/June/August/September 2024 bilateral agreements and the December 2025 policy change; it remains valid for the original Group I list but is not authoritative for the newest additions.

VERIFIED: The 8 December 2025 additions (USA, South Korea, South Africa, Bulgaria, Malta, Romania, UAE, Israel) are correctly classified as visa_free. The cancilleria.gob.bo announcement confirms they are moved to 'Grupo I (sin requerimiento de visa)'. No specific max stay stated in announcement; 90-day Group I default applies.

VERIFIED India (IND) as visa_required: India is classified in Grupo II under Decreto Supremo 27150 (Annexo II, item 54) and Bolivia–India bilateral agreement of February 2025 covers only diplomatic/official passports (confirmed by Indian Embassy La Paz eoilapaz.gov.in). Visa-on-arrival for Group II ended July 25 2025. Ordinary Indian passport holders must obtain a visa in advance; India is correctly absent from all visa_policy arrays. Airline aggregator claim of 'visa-free or visa-on-arrival' is false. Source: https://www.eoilapaz.gov.in/page/india-bolivia/
gaps: Russia entry: max_stay_days=90 confirmed by secondary sources (embassies.net) but not stated verbatim in the cancilleria.gob.bo 2016 announcement text; passport type coverage (ordinary vs all types) also unspecified in retrieved announcement.
Russia entry: The 2016 agreement may have been suspended or modified; current applicability should be verified against a post-2022 official Bolivian source given geopolitical context.

Maximum stay days for newly visa-exempt countries (US, Korea, UAE, Israel, Bulgaria, Malta, Romania, South Africa) not explicitly stated in the December 2025 cancilleria.gob.bo announcement; 90 days assumed consistent with Group I defaults.

Suriname: Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia aggregation of Bolivia's visa policy but no official Bolivian government source (cancilleria.gob.bo, migracion.gob.bo) was found confirming this; omitted pending official confirmation.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — blanket diplomatic/official/service passport rule (90-day, all countries): widely reported by Timatic but not confirmed verbatim on a directly-accessed official Bolivian government page beyond specific bilateral agreements for Equatorial Guinea, Switzerland, and Honduras.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — China diplomatic/service passport 30-day rule: prior record flagged this as unconfirmed; entry has been removed as the only source was Wikipedia (source_official=false).

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — no official Bolivian government source was found granting visa-free, visa-on-arrival, or e-visa access to Bolivia based on holding a US, Schengen, UK, Canadian, Australian, or other third-country visa or residence permit.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — no OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or analogous diaspora/heritage special status rule was found for Bolivia on any official source.
No official minimum investment amount was found for the investor/business residency path on official government sources.
No digital nomad visa, startup visa, or fast-track talent program was found on official sources.
San Marino not listed explicitly on the embassy EU page; omitted due to lack of official confirmation.
The SIGEMIG online registration system is reported as mandatory for all visitors; this is an entry requirement but not a visa/ETA — no official source was directly verified for this requirement.

Group II and Group III country lists sourced from embassy EU page which predates multiple 2024 bilateral agreements; the post-2024 Group II/III lists have not been re-verified on a current official source.

outbound_official.note: No official Bolivia government outbound visa-list page located. Bolivia's Cancillería (cancilleria.gob.bo) publishes individual press releases for each new bilateral visa-suppression agreement (e.g. Honduras March 2024, El Salvador June 2024, Dominican Republic August 2024, 8-country expansion December 2025) but maintains no single authoritative page listing all countries where Bolivian ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa — equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin does not exist for Bolivia. The visa-application portal visas.cancilleria.gob.bo and the servicios/visas pages all address inbound (foreigners entering Bolivia) requirements only. Rely on destination-side data and bilateral-agreement announcements on cancilleria.gob.bo.
